steps.title: Start the creation story
steps.content: The student wants to begin a narrative about the creation of Demir's robots. A strong opening should introduce the inspiration and the design goal before moving into details about testing, refinement, or how the finished robots operate.

steps.title: Avoid later-stage details
steps.content: Choice A explains the robots' operation, Choice B jumps to the end of the yearlong process, and Choice D focuses mainly on ants. The useful opening connects ant bridge behavior to Demir setting out to design collaborative robots. The answer is C.
